SEPARATE STATEMENT OF ITEMS IN DISPUTE IN SUPPORT OF DEFT'S OPPOSITION TO PLTF'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO PTLF'S DISCOVERY REQUESTS AND AWARD OF MONETARY SANCTIONS FILED BY DEFENDANT RAJHBOY, ROMANIE MARIE C.

DISCOVERY 610, APPEARANCES: R. MICHAEL LIEBERMAN FOR PLAINTIFF AND MOVING PARTY: CHARLES BRADLEY PORT-JONES LINDA A. RITCHIE, FOR DEFENDANT AND OPPOSING PARTY: ROMANIE C. RAJHBOY HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND ANSWERS TO DEPOSITION QUESTIONS IS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED GOOD CAUSE TO COMPEL THE DISCLOSURE OF PRIVATE MEDICAL INFORMATION. THE MERE FACT THAT THE DEFENDANT WEARS CORRECTIVE LENSES, WITHOUT MORE, DOES NOT ESTABLISH GOOD CAUSE TO BELIEVE THAT THE REQUESTED INFORMATION IS RELEVANT TO THE CAUSE OF THE ACCIDENT. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
